resources.description: An open source video and media CMS for building a YouTube-like platform. It supports uploading, transcoding, and managing video content with a modern UI.

resources.title: Livepeer
resources.description: A decentralized live video streaming platform and network built on blockchain. Livepeer provides an open source protocol for scalable, affordable live streaming.

resources.title: mediasoup
resources.description: A modern WebRTC SFU (Selective Forwarding Unit) for Node.js that enables building multi-party video conferencing and streaming applications with low latency.

resources.title: Janus WebRTC Server
resources.description: An open source WebRTC server (SFU and gateway) that allows the creation of videoconferencing, streaming, and other real-time multimedia applications.

resources.title: OvenMediaEngine
resources.description: A real-time streaming server with sub-second latency support. OvenMediaEngine offers WebRTC and Low Latency DASH/HLS for interactive live streaming.

resources.title: MistServer
resources.description: An open source streaming media server that supports multiple protocols (HLS, RTMP, WebRTC, etc.). It focuses on easy setup and compatibility with any device.

resources.title: Owncast
resources.description: A self-hosted live video streaming server with a built-in chat. Owncast enables anyone to set up a live stream akin to Twitch, but on their own infrastructure.

resources.title: PeerTube
resources.description: A decentralized video sharing platform powered by ActivityPub and peer-to-peer (BitTorrent) directly in the browser, as a federated alternative to centralized sites.

resources.title: WebTorrent
resources.description: A streaming torrent client for the web. WebTorrent uses WebRTC for peer-to-peer transport and allows in-browser video streaming from torrents without plugins.

resources.title: QCTools
resources.description: Quality Control Tools for Video Preservation – an open source toolset (by BAVC) to analyze digitized video files, with visualizations for signal quality issues.

resources.title: Natron
resources.description: A free and open source node-based compositing software, similar to After Effects or Nuke, for visual effects and motion graphics.

resources.title: Untrunc
resources.description: A tool to recover and repair corrupted video files (e.g., missing headers) by reconstructing them using a reference file of the same format.

resources.title: v4l (Go Video4Linux)
resources.description: A Go library providing bindings to Video4Linux2 (V4L2) APIs, enabling video capture and camera control in Linux from Go applications.

resources.title: mp4ff
resources.description: A Golang library and set of tools for parsing and writing MP4 (ISO BMFF) files, including support for fragmented MP4 and progressive MP4.

resources.title: gortsplib
resources.description: A pure Go RTSP server and client library that supports handling RTSP streams without external dependencies, ideal for building streaming servers or proxies.

resources.title: goav
resources.description: Go bindings for FFmpeg's libav* libraries. It allows developers to leverage FFmpeg's powerful audio and video processing features in Go applications.

resources.title: GMF (Go Media Framework)
resources.description: A Go binding for FFmpeg that simplifies working with multimedia in Go, providing high-level access to FFmpeg's decoding and encoding functionality.

resources.title: go-astiav
resources.description: A comprehensive Go binding for FFmpeg's libav* libraries, facilitating the building of media applications in Go with FFmpeg's capabilities.

resources.title: go-astits
resources.description: A Golang library for parsing and demuxing MPEG Transport Stream (.ts) files natively, enabling inspection and processing of TS files.

resources.title: go-astisub
resources.description: A Golang library for manipulating subtitles (supports formats like SRT, WebVTT, SSA/ASS, etc.), useful for automated subtitle processing.

resources.title: FFmpeg-go
resources.description: A Golang binding for FFmpeg that enables developers to generate and run FFmpeg commands programmatically in Go.

resources.title: PyFFmpeg
resources.description: A Python wrapper for FFmpeg, allowing the composition and execution of FFmpeg commands through a fluent Python interface.

resources.title: FFmpegKit
resources.description: A complete, cross-platform wrapper for FFmpeg, providing ready-to-use binaries and libraries for Android, iOS, Linux, Windows, and more.

resources.title: StaxRip
resources.description: A versatile video encoding GUI for Windows, utilizing a variety of open source tools (AviSynth/VapourSynth, x264/x265, etc.) for advanced encoding workflows.

resources.title: Shutter Encoder
resources.description: A free video converter based on FFmpeg that offers a user-friendly interface for transcoding, repackaging, and editing media files.

resources.title: HandBrake
resources.description: A popular open source video transcoder that converts video from nearly any format to a selection of modern, widely supported codecs.

resources.title: Avidemux
resources.description: A simple, free video editor for cutting, filtering, and encoding tasks, supporting many file types and codecs.

resources.title: Subtitle Edit
resources.description: A free, open source subtitle editor that supports over 200 subtitle formats, with tools for sync, translation, and conversion.

resources.title: Aegisub
resources.description: A free, cross-platform open source tool for creating and modifying subtitles (advanced subtitle editor supporting ASS/SSA formats).

resources.title: React Native Video
resources.description: A React Native component for video playback that supports both iOS and Android using native player APIs.

resources.title: Kodi
resources.description: A free and open source media center software that plays videos, music, and more across multiple platforms (formerly XBMC).

resources.title: Video Speed Controller (Chrome Extension)
resources.description: A popular browser extension that allows users to speed up or slow down video playback on any website with HTML5 video.

resources.title: ReactPlayer
resources.description: A React component for playing a variety of URLs, including YouTube, Vimeo, SoundCloud, and many more.

resources.title: Clappr
resources.description: An open source HTML5 video player that supports custom plugins and playback of various formats.

resources.title: Unified Streaming Features Page
resources.description: A demonstration of Unified Streaming's feature set, including on-the-fly packaging and multi-format support.

resources.title: Unified Streaming Demos
resources.description: A showcase of Unified Streaming's capabilities, including adaptive streaming formats and DRM-protected playback.

resources.title: Man in the Middle Proxy
resources.description: An interactive HTTPS proxy that allows inspection, modification, and debugging of network traffic, useful for video streaming analysis.

resources.title: Chrome Media Internals
resources.description: A built-in Chrome tool that provides detailed logs and diagnostics for media playback and streaming issues.

resources.title: ThreeFive SCTE35
resources.description: An SCTE-35 parser and decoder for analyzing ad markers in MPEG-TS streams, designed for broadcast workflows.

resources.title: Middleman SCTE35 104 Parser
resources.description: A web-based tool to parse and decode SCTE-35 markers for ad insertion and broadcast signaling analysis.

resources.title: M2amedia SCTE35 Dump for MPEGTS
resources.description: M2A Media's tool for extracting SCTE-35 markers from MPEG-TS streams.

resources.title: Dash Sequences GPAC Wiki
resources.description: The GPAC Wiki provides DASH sequences for conformance testing and validation.

resources.title: Dash IF Test Assets Database
resources.description: A database of test assets provided by the DASH Industry Forum for validating DASH implementations.

resources.title: BBC R&D Adaptive Bitrate Test Media
resources.description: The BBC Research & Development department offers MPEG-DASH test media for developers and researchers.

resources.title: Common Encryption - ISO/IEC 23001-7
resources.description: Defines encryption of media supported for receiving devices

resources.title: TTML/WebVTT Coding of audio-visual objects - ISO/IEC 14496-30
resources.description: Defines formatting for text tracks webvtt or ttml

resources.title: AVC/HEVC in ISOBMFF - ISO/IEC 14496-15
resources.description: Defines the carriage of AVC/HEVC in ISOBMFF

resources.title: AAC Audio Coding Standard - ISO/IEC 14496-3
resources.description: Defines the compressed audio format AAC

resources.title: HEVC/h265 Video Coding Standard - ISO/IEC 23008-2
resources.description: Defines the compressed video format HEVC/h265

resources.title: AVC/h264 Video Coding Standard - ISO/IEC 14496-10
resources.description: Defines the compressed video format for AVC/h264

resources.title: MPEG DASH - ISO/IEC 23009-01
resources.description: Defines both the metadata structure and associated media format(s)

resources.title: Dashif Content Protection List
resources.description: A list of content protection schemes recognized by the DASH Industry Forum for securing DASH streaming content.

resources.title: Dashif Conformance
resources.description: The DASH Industry Forum provides a conformance tool to validate MPEG-DASH content against the DASH standard.

resources.title: GPAC MP4Box
resources.description: MP4Box is a multimedia packager from the GPAC framework, used for encoding and packaging media files.

resources.title: Akamai Players
resources.description: Akamai provides a suite of media players optimized for high-quality streaming across various devices.

resources.title: Axinom DRM Decoders
resources.description: Axinom offers DRM tools for encoding and decoding, ensuring secure streaming media delivery.

resources.title: Mux HLS Streams
resources.description: Mux offers a set of test HLS streams, primarily used by hls.js and hosted by Mux, for testing and development.

resources.title: Eyevinn Streaming Samples
resources.description: A collection of HTTP streaming samples curated by Eyevinn, available for testing and development.

resources.title: Apple Reference Streams
resources.description: Apple provides example streams demonstrating the features of HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) for testing and development purposes.

resources.title: Apple HLS Tools
resources.description: Apple's HLS Tools assist developers in creating and validating HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) content for Apple devices.

resources.title: Eyevinn Stream Corruptor
resources.description: Eyevinn's Stream Corruptor is a tool designed to intentionally corrupt streaming media, useful for testing error resilience in players.

resources.title: Bitmovin Test Stream
resources.description: Bitmovin's Test Player allows users to experience their HTML5 video player demo, showcasing adaptive streaming capabilities.

resources.title: Unified Streaming Validator
resources.description: Unified Streaming's Validator checks the compliance and quality of streaming content, supporting various streaming protocols.

resources.title: Dolby Stream Validator
resources.description: Dolby's Stream Validator assesses streaming content for Dolby audio and video standards, ensuring high-quality media delivery.

resources.title: Akamai Stream Validator
resources.description: Akamai's Stream Validator is a tool designed to verify the integrity and compliance of streaming media, ensuring optimal playback across devices.

resources.title: xk media library
resources.description: Scan millions of files with FFmpeg and access the metadata as a SQLite database. Also, a CLI alternative to media browsers like Plex or Jellyfin.

resources.title: VTCLab Media Analyzer
resources.description: In-browser tool that helps to analyze the internal structure of MPEG-TS and MP4/MOV files.

resources.title: soldiermoth/hlsq
resources.description: A CLI for adding some color to your HLS manifests along with some basic filtering.

resources.title: Streamlab
resources.description: Multi-format stream test tool for the browser. Brings convenience to verifying, inspecting, and troubleshooting adaptive streams and video files. A common interface on top of Shaka Player, HLS.js, and Rx-Player.

resources.title: CTA | WAVE Project
resources.description: The WAVE (Web Application Video Ecosystem) Project, hosted by the Consumer Technology Association (CTA)®, aims to improve how internet-delivered commercial video is handled on consumer electronics devices and to make it easier for content creators to distribute video to those devices.

resources.description: Tests for different rate control modes in x264. Contribute to slhck/rate-control-tests development by creating an account on GitHub.
